prompt:
Background my husband 31M has almost died twice in the last 5 years from his autoimmune disease as a result his mental health has declined. I was pregnant with our daughter the first time and have done everything I can to get him mental health support since the first time as I watched his mental health decline I have taken him to appointments, gone in with him, let him go in without me (depending on what he requested), supported him through multiple medication which didn't work, spent countless hours discussing his wants, needs and struggles. Anything he needed I was there whist also figuring out how to be a mum, working, sorting daycare and doctors and running the house with basically no support from him which I accepted because I knew he was in a bad place. 

Early 2020 he changed jobs because of covid, autoimmune disorders and working with the public in a pandemic big no no so he switched to something that he had always wanted to do even though he knew he would be home less and more would be on me and I 100% supported this decision and did everything in my power to make his life as easy as possible. Late 2020 he tells me that he wants another baby. We had agreed that our daughter was going to be our only child and I was not prepared to have another baby but I told him I needed some time to come to terms with the idea and then I'm sure we could figure something out. He asks me to go off my contraception so my hormones have time to chill before I get pregnant which I agree to under the stipulation that he was careful. The first time we did anything after I had my contraception removed after being reminded multiple times throughout the act that I was not ready for a baby yet he made no attempt to be careful. I was utterly devastated and spent the rest of the month to shocked to do anything. End of the month came and guess what I'm pregnant. The day I find out he withdraws all support from me anything I had left is gone. No shoulder to cry on, he wouldn't even talk to me about how I was feeling and just took it as an attack on him. 

I am broken. When work finds out I break down and have to leave my shift because I am utterly devastated. I can't function or think. 

Finally about half way through my pregnancy I completely break and have a full blown breakdown that almost cost me and my son our lives. He spent the entire time at a mates place with our daughter and only called for help when he noticed I had cut the seat belt out of my car and taken all my meds with me. I spend 4 days in hospital and my husband and I discussed how things had gotten to that point and how we were going to improve things in the future. 

I get out of hospital and the next day my husband disappears on me to his mates place which I later find out is because he met a 19 year old there when I had my break down and is hanging with her. That night he sleeps with her then proceeds to do a 180 flip on who he had been for our entire relationship turning his back on our daughter spending all his time with this teenager whist telling me he only saw her a couple of times, being disrespectful to me and continuing to lie, cheat and worse. The whole time I knew I could feel it. I could feel that he was lying to me. Eventually one night I know he is lying to me after 5 weeks and I ring the girl and fill her in on what's been happening in our house things like that we were still sleeping together and he was telling me he wanted to be with me and she was the worst mistake of his life. She is shocked but young and still wants to be with my husband. He takes off and sits on his own for ages before eventually ending up at her place for one last go before coming home to me and saying he wanted to fix things and start over and breaking up with her. I said no. There is no housing in this area so we still live together and for the last almost year he has been no where but work, shops and home to try and show me that he is serious. He is back to treating me and our family with respect he is following up on his mental health of his own accord, he worked really hard on forming a bond with our son because they just weren't connected like he was with my daughter, he supported me through labour and delivery ( only one person in the room because of covid and I respect that he is the children's parent), he wants to make some form of a relationship work in the future and completely understands that what happened was beyond unacceptable. 

I feel that even if we got back together eventually our vows were broken so the marriage is void and I don't want to stay in a marriage for the possibility of getting back together one day. I will never trust him and as I have explained to him the lid is on the box of  my emotions because it's to painful to feel them (PTSD response from my younger years) he is trying everyday and he has respected that I will not even accept a slightly raised voice or wrong word without asking him to leave at least to think about what he has done and come back to me with a coping strategy to ensure it doesn't happen again. I won't accept that behaviour in my house from our 4 year old so why would I accept it from him. Over the years I'm sure it would probably be easier with the children to be in some form of mutually respectful relationship but I don't see that having a marriage certificate contributes to that and I also know that if he loves me and I am incapable of feeling anything for him that is eventually going to mess with him and our children's view of a normal relationship. I know I should probably eventually learn how to take the lids off the feelings boxes but I'm not there yet. 

So today we can legally get divorced and I was like yep let's do this which I have been saying for the last year and he turns around and says do we have to I really thought you might change your mind or at least put it off for a bit to figure out where we end up. 

Honestly just any comments on this whole messed up situation and how to cope moving forward and any advice on if I should apply for divorce would be greatly appreciated.

TL;DR: husband has a breakdown as a response to mine turns into a deadbeat then works really hard on sorting his shit what do I do?Sorry I forgot to give context on who he was before. 

He was the man that planned our wedding because he could see that I was getting overwhelmed and wanted me to have the ultimate wedding without all the stress so he asked me what I dreamed of and then came to me with options that fit my dream he also said that it didn't matter to him as long as he got to marry me at the end of it. 

He was the man that saw me struggling with skin irritations to finally find a moisturiser that worked but it wasn't available online and the closest store was two hours away so he drove up to buy as much of it as he could get so I never had to deal with the pain and irritation again. 

He was the man that sat by my bed side when I lost my first two pregnancies and held me whist I cried for weeks even though he had his own grief 

He was the man that was always there when ever any challenge or obstacle was in my way to knock it down with me. 

He was my other half which is why after 5 years of being together and him literally having my back for every day of it I agreed to marry him. 

He taught me to drive when it terrified me and would stay up late on work nights so we could spend half an hour driving round when the streets were empty 

He would cook me dinner if I worked a late shift and come home on his lunch break to make me coffee and breakfast before I had to go to work 

He would wake me up with coffee and breakfast almost every day if he could 

I was his queen and he treated me like that. It started to change after the first time he almost died but got much worse after the second time. Until then he treated me with all the love in the world and our daughter was his princess she could never want for anything and he loved her beyond belief. 

I hope that helps with some insight on why I said 180 flip.
response:
Thank you for sharing your story. It sounds like you've been through an incredibly challenging and painful journey. Your feelings of betrayal, hurt, and mistrust are completely valid given what you've experienced. Here are some thoughts and advice on your situation:

1. **Trust and Betrayal**: Trust is a fundamental part of any relationship, and once it's broken, it can be incredibly difficult to rebuild. Your husband's actions, particularly during your pregnancy and his infidelity, have understandably shattered your trust. It's important to acknowledge that rebuilding trust takes time, effort, and a genuine commitment from both parties. However, if you feel that you will never be able to trust him again, that is a significant barrier to continuing the marriage.

2. **Your Well-being**: Your mental and emotional well-being should be a priority. You've been through a lot, and it's crucial to take care of yourself. If you haven't already, consider seeking therapy or counseling to help process your emotions and experiences. This can also help you work through the PTSD and any other lingering trauma.

3. **Divorce Consideration**: If you feel that the marriage is void due to broken vows and that staying married is not in your best interest, then pursuing a divorce might be the right decision for you. It's important to make a decision that aligns with your values and what you believe is best for your future and your children's well-being.

4. **Co-Parenting**: Regardless of your marital status, maintaining a respectful and cooperative co-parenting relationship is crucial for your children's stability and happiness. It sounds like your husband is making efforts to be a better father, which is positive for your children. Focus on creating a healthy environment for them, whether or not you remain married.

5. **Future Relationships**: It's understandable that you might not be ready to open the "box" of emotions right now. Healing takes time, and it's okay to take things at your own pace. When you're ready, working through these emotions can help you move forward, whether that's in a relationship with your husband or someone else in the future.

6. **Communication**: Continue to communicate openly with your husband about your needs and boundaries. It's important for him to understand where you stand and what you need from him, whether that's as a co-parent or in any other capacity.

Ultimately, the decision to divorce or not is deeply personal and should be based on what you believe is best for you and your children. Trust your instincts and

Leaving your fiancée while she was in rehab is a complex situation, and it's understandable that you're struggling with feelings of guilt and betrayal. However, it's important to consider a few key points:

1. **Communication and Trust:** Your fiancée hid a significant part of her life from you, which understandably shattered your trust. Trust is foundational in any relationship, and her secrecy about her drinking problem was a major breach. It's reasonable to feel hurt and betrayed by this.

2. **Her Effort to Seek Help:** On the other hand, your fiancée recognized she had a problem and took the initiative to seek help. This is a significant step and shows a level of responsibility and self-awareness. It's not easy for someone struggling with addiction to admit they need help, and she did that.

3. **Your Reaction:** Your decision to leave while she was in rehab might seem harsh, but it's also a reflection of how deeply her actions affected you. It's okay to prioritize your own emotional well-being and to step back from a situation that feels overwhelming or damaging.

4. **Guilt and Regret:** Feeling guilty about leaving her is natural, especially since she was taking steps to improve her life. However, it's important to remember that you also have to take care of yourself. If the trust is too damaged for you to continue the relationship, that's a valid reason to step away.

5. **Future Considerations:** If you're considering getting back together, it's crucial to have open and honest conversations about what happened, how it affected you both, and what you need moving forward. Rebuilding trust takes time and effort from both parties.

Ultimately, you're not "scum" for leaving. You're a person who was deeply hurt and reacted in a way that made sense to you at the time. It's important to reflect on what you want and need in a relationship and whether you believe those needs can be met with her moving forward. If you decide to reconnect, it should be with clear boundaries and expectations to ensure both of you can heal and grow.

prompt:
AITA for being rude and ignoring my mother after she picked multiple fights with me for being gay? This isn’t exactly recent but isn’t completely old later. This took place around 2 months ago. I’m still considered a minor and am female for some background information. 

So around two months ago I decided to come out to my mother as gay. At first she seemed fine with it and it really didn’t seem too bad. However, the next day I decided to tell her that in the past I had dated a girl. She immediately flipped out and started calling me manipulative, abusive, a liar, and many other things along the lines of a traitor and a fake. This went on for almost 5 days, and I got fed up with her behavior. So I went ahead and told her this was really disgusting behavior and she wasn’t acting like a parent; she told me I was being an arrogant teenager. 

Fast forward a week or so and everything seems fine. Until she starts nitpicking at everything I do. Didn’t tell her about a friend? She’d scream at me, didn’t eat all of my dinner or lunch? Another screamfest. This got so bad that I started locking myself away from her because I couldn’t take all of this.

Fast forward another couple of weeks and she calls me and arrogant bitch for not giving her a hug? I know I may have come off as rude because I was moody that day and I didn’t ask how her work day was but I don’t think it warranted that reaction. 

An instance soon after that happened where one of my teachers found out about my condition and called the counselor. The counselor had to notify my mom and then she talked to me. 

She said that I had caused all the fights with her by being rude and arrogant and she never had a problem with me being gay. And that I was troubled because I had a somewhat troubled past. I got fed up and told her I was locking myself away because of her bad parenting skills and the fact she wasn’t raising me right. Am I the asshole? She’s been acting alright for almost a week now but I still feel contempt against her and I know that at times I can be moody and rude. And I know that she did do a lot for me, I’m just not sure who’s right here.
response:
You are not the asshole for feeling hurt and upset by your mother's reaction to your coming out and her subsequent behavior. It's understandable that you would feel the need to distance yourself from someone who is treating you poorly, especially when it comes to something as personal and important as your sexual orientation.

Your mother's initial reaction and her continued behavior were not supportive or respectful. Calling you manipulative and abusive for sharing your truth is not acceptable, and it's not okay for her to blame you for the fights when her actions were clearly hurtful. It's also concerning that she resorted to name-calling and yelling over minor issues, which suggests a lack of healthy communication.

It's important to acknowledge that while you may have moments of moodiness, as many teenagers do, it doesn't justify her behavior. You deserve to be treated with respect and understanding, especially by your parent.

If your mother is acting better now, it might be an opportunity to try to have a calm and honest conversation with her about how her actions affected you. It could be helpful to involve a counselor or therapist in these discussions to facilitate better communication and understanding between you two.

Remember, you have the right to be who you are and to be treated with love and respect. It's okay to set boundaries to protect yourself from hurtful behavior.
